title: ranunculus ollissiponensis subsp. alpinus
intro: Ranunculus ollissiponensis subsp. alpinus, also known as the Oregon buttercup, is a plant species in the ranunculus genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits associated with Ranunculus ollissiponensis subsp. alpinus include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which may be used to treat various health issues.
